what: `post_tool_use_hook.py` (`.claude/hooks/post_tool_use_hook.py`, Phase 1) is the Claude Code `PostToolUse` command-type hook. Per the design inventory (design.md:131) and hook-wiring table (design.md:216) it "Runs lint, type check, SAST (Semgrep), wiring check on changed files; returns specific errors via stdout." Concretely (tasks.md 18.1, lines 309-316): on `Write`/`Edit`/`MultiEdit` completion it runs lint (`ruff check`) + type check (`mypy`) on the changed files, runs `semgrep --config auto` filtering for HIGH/CRITICAL findings, runs `python3 tools/wiring_checker.py` on the changed files, collects all findings, emits them to stdout as structured next-turn feedback, and exits 1 (non-blocki…

open_gaps.gap: Requirement-to-task content mismatch on type-checking. REQ-VERIFY-004 (requirements.md:189) says the hook runs 'lint, SAST, and wiring checks' — type-checking (mypy) is NOT named in the requirement, but the design hook-wiring row (design.md:216) adds 'type check' and task 18.1 (tasks.md:311) adds `mypy`. The authoritative EARS criterion under-specifies what the hook actually does.
open_gaps.fix: Amend REQ-VERIFY-004 to read 'run lint, type-check, SAST, and wiring checks' so the requirement, design row, and task agree, OR drop mypy from task 18.1 if type-check is meant to be Verifier-only.

open_gaps.gap: Audit-log producer NOT wired into post_tool_use_hook.py while the other comparable hooks are. REQ-AUDIT-001 (requirements.md:460) and task 52.3 (tasks.md:970) wire audit_log.append(...) into stop_hook.py, pre_tool_use_hook.py, and subagent_stop_hook.py 'on every allow/block decision' — PostToolUse is deliberately excluded. This is internally consistent (PostToolUse makes no allow/block decision), but means PostToolUse findings/feedback events are NOT in the tamper-evident gate-decision log, even though task 36.1 DOES forward them to OTel. So the audit log and the OTel trace disagree on whether PostToolUse events are recorded.
open_gaps.fix: State explicitly in design.md (inventory row design.md:131 and audit section) that PostToolUse is intentionally NOT an audit-log producer because it issues no gate decision, and reconcile with task 36.1 by clarifying that OTel forwarding of PostToolUse is informational telemetry, not an audit-logged decision.

open_gaps.gap: Design/tasks thinness. The component has NO dedicated design subsection (unlike stop_hook.py's 'No-Progress Watchdog' and pre_tool_use_hook.py's 'plan-approved.json Gate' sections). Its entire design coverage is three table rows (inventory design.md:131, hook-wiring design.md:216, error-handling design.md:813) plus one sequence-diagram line (105). No pseudocode, no defined output schema for the 'structured feedback' it emits to stdout, no specification of HOW changed files are determined (the hook receives a PostToolUse event — how it extracts the file path(s) from Write/Edit/MultiEdit payloads is unspecified), and no spec for what 'structured feedback' format the agent consumes.
open_gaps.fix: Add a short design subsection defining: (a) how changed-file paths are parsed from the PostToolUse event payload, (b) the JSON/text schema of the structured feedback emitted to stdout, (c) how multi-tool MultiEdit batches are handled. Mirror the depth of the stop-hook/pre-hook sections.

open_gaps.gap: No timeout specified for the hook despite shelling out to four potentially-slow external processes (ruff, mypy, semgrep --config auto, wiring_checker.py). The Req 20.1 'hook validator timeout of 60 seconds' (requirements.md:354) is documented as applying to spec_validator.py (design.md:814) and is silent on post_tool_use_hook.py. A semgrep --config auto run on a large changeset can exceed 60s; the spec gives no timeout behavior for this hook.
open_gaps.fix: Specify in task 18.1 / design that post_tool_use_hook.py applies the 60s validator-timeout default per-tool (or a dedicated budget), and on timeout still exits 1 with a 'post-hook timeout' finding — never blocks.

open_gaps.gap: REQ-OBS-003 hook-decision forwarding conceptually mis-fits PostToolUse. Task 36.1 (tasks.md:540) sets attribute 'decision' (allow/block) on the emitted span for 'all six hooks' including PostToolUse — but PostToolUse never makes an allow/block decision (it always exits 1, non-blocking). REQ-OBS-003 (requirements.md:236) is scoped to 'WHEN a hook makes a gate decision'. PostToolUse makes none, so either the requirement's trigger does not apply to it or the 'decision' attribute is meaningless/empty for this hook.
open_gaps.fix: Either exclude PostToolUse from the REQ-OBS-003 gate-decision forwarding (it is not a gate), or define a distinct span event type (e.g. hook.feedback with a findings count) for PostToolUse rather than reusing the allow/block 'decision' attribute.

open_gaps.gap: Zero formal-verification coverage. No Z3 CHECK pins this component (confirmed: grep of formal_verification_merged.py for post_tool_use/lint/sast/wiring → no match). Its single load-bearing invariant — 'PostToolUse MUST exit 1 / must never block / cannot undo' — is asserted only in prose (requirements.md:251) and a Hypothesis PBT (Property 21, generic across all hooks). Given the spec's heavy reliance on the harness for every other gate invariant, the absence of any machine-checked 'PostToolUse-never-exits-2' assertion is a coverage gap relative to the rest of the system.
open_gaps.fix: Add a CHECK to formal_verification_merged.py modeling postHookBlocks ⇒ False (i.e. a PostToolUse path that exits 2 is UNSAT), parallel to CHECK-5b/5c/8c that pin the Stop-hook exit-0 HANDOFF invariant, so the prevention/feedback split is formally pinned on both sides.

open_gaps.gap: Semgrep vs Semgrep_CodeQL naming. The hook runs only 'Semgrep' (design.md:216, tasks.md:312), but the glossary term Semgrep_CodeQL (requirements.md:47) and REQ-VERIFY-001 (requirements.md:186) pair Semgrep with CodeQL for SAST. The in-loop hook deliberately runs only Semgrep (CodeQL is a CI workflow, task 23); this is reasonable but nowhere stated, so a reader expecting 'SAST' to mean Semgrep+CodeQL would assume the hook runs CodeQL too.
open_gaps.fix: Note in the hook-wiring row / task 18.1 that PostToolUse runs Semgrep ONLY (CodeQL is the CI gate per task 23 / REQ-SEC-001) so the 'SAST' wording is not misread as including CodeQL at edit time.

critic_findings.detail: Property 21's requirement-mapping line (tasks.md:591) lists '18.1' as one of the Requirements it validates: '_Requirements: 5.5, 7.4, 7.5, 10.2, 13.5, 14.1, 14.2, 18.1_'. Task 18.1 IS the post_tool_use_hook.py implementation. So the PBT suite explicitly traces Property 21 back to this hook's task — meaning the spec DOES wire a property-based test to post_tool_use_hook.py via task 18.1, not merely 'generically across all hooks.' The draft's properties_and_checks bullet says Property 21 'is the ONLY numbered Property that constrains this hook, and only via the shared exit-code contract' but never notes that tasks.md:591 names task 18.1 by number in Property 21's requirement list, which is the …

Design-vs-design internal contradiction on AST analysis the draft never flags. Req 9.1/REQ-VERIFY-001 (requirements.md:186) and the Verifier section (design.md:258) and task 17.x (tasks.md:215) all define the STRUCTURAL layer as 'lint, type check, AST analysis/AST'. The draft maps PostToolUse as a 'fast-feedback shadow of layers 1+4' (structural+security) and says it runs lint+type+SAST+wiring. But the structural layer's third element — AST analysis — is NOT in the PostToolUse task (task 18.1 runs ruff+mypy+semgrep+wiring_checker, no standalone AST step), even though wiring_checker.py itself uses the ast module (task 19.1, tasks.md:327). The draft asserts the hook is a 'shadow of layers 1+4'…
critic_findings.cit: requirements.md:186; design.md:258; tasks.md:215; tasks.md:327; cf. draft ears_stories REQ-VERIFY-001 row

critic_findings.detail: The draft's REQ-VERIFY-004 ears_story cites the requirement as 'requirements.md:189' and labels its req_id 'REQ-VERIFY-004 (Req 9.4)', but does not verify that task 18.1's own back-reference uses the bare numeric form '9.4' (tasks.md:316: '_Requirements: 9.4, 13.2_') rather than the REQ-VERIFY-004 PRD id. This is the canonical traceability link for the component and it is numeric-only. More importantly, the draft never checks that REQ-VERIFY-004 is acceptance-criterion #4 under Requirement 9 (requirements.md:189) — i.e. the '9.4' mapping is correct — but it ALSO never notes that the requirement text at 189 omits BOTH 'type check' AND 'AST analysis' that appear downstream, so the under-specif…

Postgres-unavailable fallback obligation (N-25, tasks.md:730) is not assessed for PostToolUse. The reconciliation note at tasks.md:730 requires that 'every gate (Stop coverage gate, PreToolUse status/integrity guards, OPA merge policy) MUST fall back to file-backed state ... never fail-open' when db_connection.health_check() fails. PostToolUse is correctly NOT in that gate list (it makes no gate decision), but task 36.1 makes PostToolUse emit an OTel span, and if telemetry/DB is down the hook must still exit 1 and not crash the edit loop. The draft's fail-open-on-crash behavior covers exceptions generically but does not connect to the N-25 'never fail-open' family invariant to explicitly sta…
critic_findings.cit: tasks.md:730; tasks.md:540; cf. draft behaviors 'Fail-open on its own crash'

critic_findings.detail: The draft's config_settings entry 'hook type = command' gives owner 'settings.json / REQ-STEER-004' and default note '(never http/mcp — fail-closed family requirement, though this hook itself never blocks)'. REQ-STEER-004 (requirements.md:253) mandates command-type hooks specifically 'for hard policy enforcement (which fail closed)' and says the system SHALL NOT use HTTP/MCP 'for hard policy.' PostToolUse enforces NO hard policy (it never blocks), so REQ-STEER-004's command-type mandate does not strictly bind it the way it binds the fail-closed hooks — the design.md:203 blanket statement ('All hooks are command type ... so they fail closed') is the actual source, and it conflates this non-bl…
critic_findings.cit: requirements.md:253; design.md:203; cf. draft config_settings 'hook type = command'

critic_findings.detail: Integration test 26.1 mapping detail omitted. The draft cites tasks.md:409 for the exit-1 integration assertion but does not surface that test 26.1's requirement trace (tasks.md:410: '_Requirements: 8.1, 8.2, 9.4, 10.3, 13.2_') ties the PostToolUse integration assertion to Req 8.1/8.2 (wiring) and 9.4 (verify) and 13.2 (steer) simultaneously — confirming the spec treats the hook's wiring-check behavior as testably bound to Req 8, not just Req 9.4. The draft's Req-8.1 ears_story is marked 'partial' citing only tasks.md:313 and 325-331 (the wiring_checker engine) and misses that integration test 26.1 actually exercises the hook+wiring_checker path together under Req 8.1/8.2 — a stronger covera…
critic_findings.cit: tasks.md:404-410 (test 26.1, _Requirements: 8.1, 8.2, 9.4, ...); cf. draft ears_stories Req-8.1 row

critic_findings.detail: The draft's open_gaps correctly notes 'no spec for how changed files are determined,' but understates the evidence: independent grep confirms the spec has a concrete pattern it FAILS to apply here. subagent_stop_hook.py task 178 explicitly says 'Parse subagent result from stdin (JSON); locate evidence field(s)' (tasks.md:178) and pre_tool_use_hook.py reads tool input — i.e. sibling hooks document their stdin/payload parsing, but task 18.1 (tasks.md:309-316) says only 'on changed files' with no stdin-parsing step at all. So the gap is not merely 'unspecified in the abstract' — it is a documented inconsistency where every comparable hook specifies its input-parsing and this one uniquely does n…

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL verify each Slice across five layers: structural (lint, type checking, AST analysis), semantic..., behavioral..., security (SAST via Semgrep_CodeQL), and performance + accessibility...
ears_needing_work.how: PostToolUse runs the STRUCTURAL (lint/type) + SECURITY (Semgrep SAST) + wiring subset on every edit as in-loop feedback; the AUTHORITATIVE five-layer verification (incl. semantic/behavioral/perf/a11y and the proof-producing run) is the Verifier subagent's job, not the hook's. Hook is a fast-feedback…

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL run static call-graph and dead-code analysis on changed files and SHALL represent each wiring obligation as a WIRING coverage item in feature_list.json.
ears_needing_work.how: PostToolUse invokes wiring_checker.py on changed files (task 18.1, line 313); wiring_checker (task 19) does the AST call-graph/dead-code analysis and emits WIRING item candidates to stdout. The hook is the trigger; wiring_checker is the engine.

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN a hook makes a gate decision, THE System SHALL emit that decision — event, tool, allow/block, reason, requirement ID — to the same trace endpoint as agent spans.
ears_needing_work.how: Task 36.1 extends 'all six hooks (Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SubagentStop, SessionStart, PreCompact)' to emit OTel gate-decision events. PostToolUse is named in the six. Note: PostToolUse makes no allow/block 'gate decision' (it never blocks), so what it forwards is a feedback/finding event, not…

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL enforce the following DEFAULT numeric thresholds: ...0 HIGH or CRITICAL SAST findings...
ears_needing_work.how: PostToolUse filters semgrep output for HIGH/CRITICAL findings (task 18.1 line 312) — but only REPORTS them (exit 1), it does not enforce/block on them. Enforcement of the 0-HIGH/CRITICAL threshold is the CI SAST gate (REQ-SEC-001, CodeQL/Semgrep workflows), not this hook.

citation_issues: design.md:131 misattribution — The draft's 'what' section quotes 'Runs lint, type check, SAST (Semgrep), wiring check on changed files; returns specific errors via stdout' and attributes it jointly to '(design.md:131) and hook-wiring table (design.md:216)'. The actual text at design.md:131 (component inventory row) reads: 'Runs lint + SAST + wiring checks after each file edit; returns errors as next-turn feedback.' It omits 'type check', omits '(Semgrep)', and says 'next-turn feedback' not 'specific errors via stdout'. The verbatim quoted formulation exists ONLY at design.md:216 (hook-wiring table row). design.md:131 is a partial-match misattribution — the quoted text cannot be confirmed at that line.

requirements.md:460 substance check — The draft cites 'requirements.md:460; tasks.md:970' for the open_gaps claim about audit_log.py NOT being wired into post_tool_use_hook.py. requirements.md:460 is actually Requirement 27.4 (which names stop_hook.py, pre_tool_use_hook.py, and subagent_stop_hook.py as audit-log callers and omits PostToolUse). The citation resolves and the content supports the claim, but the canonical ID for this sub-criterion is Req 27.4, not 'REQ-AUDIT-001' at that line number. REQ-AUDIT-001 appears as a PRD reference tag in the Requirement 27 header (line 456), not at line 460 itself. The citation is slightly imprecise (line 460 = sub-criterion 4, not the REQ-AUDIT-001 tag) but is not a phantom — the content at line 460 does support the claim.
